question: Within the largest top-level sub-discussion of this episode, how many direct replies express explicit skepticism that `folx' is actually used in practice? (Substantive endorsement of skepticism required; one-word reactions excluded.)
criterion_definition: Count direct replies (depth-1 children) under the largest top-level sub-discussion's root that contain a substantive claim challenging actual usage of the term `folx' in practice. Personal-experience reports of non-encounter (e.g. `I have never seen X used') count as explicit skepticism. Bare agreements (`+1', `agreed') and one-word reactions are excluded. Off-topic / stylistic / meta comments are excluded.

per_comment_annotations.hn_c_42c8a01: include  · personal-experience non-encounter (library: `personal-experience reports of non-encounter count as explicit skepticism')
per_comment_annotations.hn_c_42c8a03: include  · explicit skepticism with scope qualifier
per_comment_annotations.hn_c_42c8a05: include  · explicit non-use, framed as career observation

per_comment_annotations.hn_c_42c8a01: exclude · personal-experience report; not direct skepticism (boundary case, not sure)
per_comment_annotations.hn_c_42c8a03: include · explicit skepticism w/ scope qualifier
per_comment_annotations.hn_c_42c8a05: include · explicit non-use, framed as career observation
per_comment_annotations.hn_c_42c8a09: exclude · stylistic reaction; not about usage

reviewer_session.reasoning: 11 direct replies under the largest top-level subtree. 4 substantively claim non-use; 3 endorse use; 3 off-topic / stylistic; 1 one-liner. The 5th potential include (hn_c_42c8a01) is a personal-experience report ('I have never seen anyone use folx outside of online forums') — I read this as describing the speaker's exposure rather than direct skepticism, so excluded it. Tentative answer: A = 4.

joint_agreement.verdict: NO  ·  scope match (✓), per-comment-label mismatch on 1 node, answer mismatch (4 vs 5)

phase7_resolution.library_clause_text: Personal-experience reports of non-encounter (e.g., `I have never seen X used' or `In my whole career I've heard X once') count as explicit skepticism, because they constitute a substantive claim about actual usage rather than a bare reaction.
phase7_resolution.library_clause_origin: pilot-derived; entered into criterion library before bulk verification began

phases.name: Distractor Audit
phases.note: no fabricated distractors; all four options derived from real error_type categories
